question: What is the memory architecture of the GB200 Blackwell GPU, and how does HBM3e contribute to AI training performance?
findings: The GB200 NVL72 rack-scale system equips each Blackwell GPU with 192 GB HBM3e, aggregating up to 13.5 TB of HBM3e across 72 GPUs, with 768 GB per 4-GPU tray.
The GB200 NVL72 system comprises 36 NVIDIA Grace CPUs and 72 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs, with up to 30.5 TB of total fast-access memory (HBM3e + LPDDR5X combined).
The NVIDIA roadmap designates Blackwell (original) and Blackwell Ultra as both using 8-stack HBM3e ('8S HBM3e'), with the transition to HBM4 occurring at the Rubin generation.
The GB300 NVL72 (Blackwell Ultra) also uses HBM3e as its GPU memory type, delivering approximately 8 TB/s aggregate GPU memory bandwidth across the rack.
The GB300 NVL72 (Blackwell Ultra) features 20 TB of HBM3e with up to 576 TB/s total HBM bandwidth, delivering 18× higher HBM bandwidth versus NVIDIA HGX H100.
